title: T-012 — API: BOM list and detail (GET /api/technical/items/:code/boms[, /:version])
prompt:
# T-012 — API: BOM list and detail (GET /api/technical/items/:code/boms[, /:version])

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §7.1, §7.5
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
BOM list per item and BOM detail (flattened header+lines+co-products) endpoints.

Use Drizzle joins. Detail response keys: header, lines, co_products. Shape matches snapshot_json contract so the same renderer can be reused for snapshots viewer (TEC-025).

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- app/api/technical/items/[item_code]/boms/route.ts [create]
- app/api/technical/items/[item_code]/boms/[version]/route.ts [create]
- lib/technical/bom/queries.ts [create]
- tests/api/bom-read.test.ts [create]

## Out of scope
- BOM create/edit (T-013)
- Snapshot viewer
- Approval

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given item has BOM v1 and v2, when GET /boms, then v2 returns first.
2. Given BOM v1 has 3 lines + 1 co-product, when GET /boms/1, then JSON includes header + 3 lines + 1 co_product entry.
3. Given org B requests org A BOM, when GET runs, then 404 due to RLS.

## Test strategy
- RED tests cross-org RLS + JSON shape matches snapshot_json contract.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/api/bom-read.test.ts

## Risk red lines
- Do not bypass RLS via privileged client.
- Do not include audit-only fields in response.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
